title: Lychnophora passerina
taxonomy: Lychnophora passerina is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Brazil and is commonly known as 'erva-azeite' or 'erva-do-bugre' due to its unique properties. The plant belongs to the genus Lychnophora, which consists of about 12 species. Lychnophora passerina is a shrubby plant that grows up to 2 meters in height, producing yellow flowers in clusters. This plant is used in traditional medicine and has been studied for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lychnophora passerina include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
